name: Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop
name_original: Vía Verde de Préjanol – circular desde Arnedillo – Reserva de la Biosfera Valles del Cidacos
region: Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa
description: This difficult 22.2-mile touring cycling route from Arnedillo offers 2325 feet of elevation gain through the Valles del Cidacos Biosphere Re
region_intro: Touring cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa offers routes through diverse landscapes, including limestone mountains, lush river valleys, and Mediterranean scrubland. The region is characterized by striking eroded geological formations and features the Cidacos River, which flows through the area. Natural thermal springs are also a notable feature, particularly around Arnedillo. This area is part of the Biosphere Reserve of La Rioja, highlighting its ecological significance. Best touring cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa The most popular touring cycling route is Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop, a 22.1 miles (35.7 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route connects two greenways, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Arnedillo and thermal baths – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedillo, a moderate 12.9 miles (20.7 km) path. This route follows the Cidacos Greenway and passes by the thermal baths of Arnedillo. Local touring cyclists also love the Préjano – Préjano Castle loop from Préjano, a 16.7 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the historic Préjano Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Touring cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa is defined by converted railway paths like the Cidacos Greenway, limestone mountains, and river valleys, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll encounter routes through limestone mountains, lush river valleys, and Mediterranean scrubland. A significant feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a converted railway path that provides a gentler ride through tunnels and past striking clay-colored cliffs.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, the region has options for easier rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is particularly well-suited for families due to its gentle gradients and scenic views. For a moderate, family-friendly loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.7 km long and passes by the thermal baths.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The touring cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa cater to various skill levels. While there are 2 easy and 10 moderate routes, the majority (49 out of 61) are classified as difficult, often involving significant elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994444570/cidacos-and-prejano-greenways-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos and Préjano Greenways Loop&lt;/a&gt; are considered difficult due to their length and elevation.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical attractions. You can cycle past striking eroded geological formations, the thermal springs of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy panoramic views from high points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the ruins of the abandoned village of Turruncún.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano – Préjano Castle loop from Préjano&lt;/a&gt; is a 26.8 km route that takes you through varied terrain and past the historic castle. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Villarroya&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 60.3 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: The region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring colder weather to higher elevations.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the cycling trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails can vary, many natural areas in La Rioja are generally dog-friendly, especially on established paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved, especially in areas with wildlife or other visitors.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about this region?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique experience of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, and the opportunity to combine cycling with relaxation at the thermal baths of Arnedillo.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: The town of Arnedillo, a central point in the region, offers various services including cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation options. These can be found conveniently close to popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, making it a good base for exploring.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling areas, especially with bikes?
A: Public transport options to reach Arnedillo and surrounding areas may be limited, particularly for transporting bikes. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycles in advance. Many visitors prefer to arrive by car, which also offers more flexibility for accessing various trailheads.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route?
A: Parking is generally available in the main towns and villages such as Arnedillo and Préjano, which serve as common starting points for many routes. For instance, if you're starting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Herce&lt;/a&gt;, you would typically find parking in Herce or Arnedillo.

Q: Are there any unique natural phenomena to observe while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique geology. You can observe striking eroded rock formations and the natural thermal waters that surface around Arnedillo, reaching temperatures up to 52.5 °C. These hot springs offer free outdoor thermal pools along the Cidacos River, providing a unique hot and cold bathing experience.
tips: Enciso is in a mountainous area of the Iberian System, on the border with Soria. The Cidacos River crosses it and is known for having 3000 dinosaur footprints.
The Cidacos greenway offers you fantastic landscapes through a very smooth road with slight ups and downs. The Dinosaur Route is part of the MyMerak Route through the western area of La Rioja.

Arnedillo is a picturesque town in the middle of the Sierras de la Hez and the Sierra de Peñalmonte on the Cidacos River. A clear highlight in Arnedillo are the steaming thermal springs on the riverbank. These are freely accessible and invite you to take a wonderfully relaxing bath.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: While sections of the route utilize the generally easy Vía Verde del Cidacos and Vía Verde de Préjano, the full circular route from Arnedillo is classified as difficult for touring cyclists. It involves a significant elevation gain of about 710 meters over 35.7 km, requiring very good fitness and potentially some sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, combining sections of asphalt and compacted earth or gravel. You'll cycle through fertile valleys, past market gardens, and into more rugged mountain landscapes with rocky cliffs. The Vía Verde de Préjano section, for instance, passes through olive and almond groves.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For an experienced touring cyclist, the route is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 14 minutes to complete. However, this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or points of interest.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the charming settlement of Enciso and the well-known Cidacos Greenway. The starting point, Arnedillo and thermal baths, is also a highlight. Keep an eye out for griffon vultures in the rocky cliffs near Arnedillo, and you'll encounter two tunnels (one 550m long and lit) and bridges from the old railway line.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for challenging sections, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable cycling and better enjoyment of the natural surroundings.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde de Préjanol circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While greenways like the Vía Verde del Cidacos are often dog-friendly, this specific circular route includes more challenging, potentially unpaved sections and passes through a Biosphere Reserve. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other cyclists. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation, and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in the Reserva de la Biosfera Valles del Cidacos?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Vía Verde sections or cycle within the Reserva de la Biosfera Valles del Cidacos. However, as it is a protected area, please respect all local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve its natural and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the trailhead in Arnedillo?
faqs.answer: Arnedillo, being a popular starting point and home to thermal baths, typically offers parking options. Look for designated parking areas within the town, especially near the access points to the Vía Verde del Cidacos. It's best to arrive early, especially on weekends or during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates and intersects with several other trails. It utilizes significant portions of the Cidacos Greenway (Vía Verde del Cidacos) and the Vía Verde de Préjano. You'll also find it connects with sections of the Sierras Riojanas, Camino Natural Via Verde del rio Cidacos, Camino Navalsaz-Muro de Aguas/Préjano, and Camino de los molinos de Préjano, among others.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' classification and significant elevation, you should pack essentials for a strenuous ride. This includes plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Due to varied terrain, consider tires suitable for both asphalt and compacted gravel. A headlamp might be useful for the longer tunnel, though it is lit.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route, especially in Arnedillo?
faqs.answer: Yes, Arnedillo is a well-known village with thermal baths, so you'll find various amenities. There are cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options available in Arnedillo where you can refuel and rest before or after your ride.
